# Pyreon Framework — Complete AI Reference

> Signal-based UI framework for TypeScript. SSR, SSG, islands, SPA.
> Standard JSX + TypeScript. Fastest framework on all JS Framework Benchmark tests.
> All packages under @pyreon/* scope.

## Package Map

| Package | Import | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| @pyreon/reactivity | signal, computed, effect, onCleanup, batch, createStore, createResource, untrack | Reactive primitives |
| @pyreon/core | h, Fragment, For, Show, Switch, Match, Suspense, ErrorBoundary, lazy, Dynamic, Portal, onMount, onUnmount, onUpdate, createContext, useContext, provide, createRef, cx, splitProps, mergeProps, createUniqueId, TargetedEvent, ExtractProps, HigherOrderComponent | Component model |
| @pyreon/runtime-dom | mount, hydrateRoot, Transition, TransitionGroup, KeepAlive, SVG/MathML namespace (67 tags), custom elements | DOM renderer |
| @pyreon/runtime-server | renderToString, renderToStream, runWithRequestContext, Suspense 30s timeout, XSS-safe templates, For key markers | SSR renderer |
| @pyreon/router | createRouter, RouterProvider, RouterView, RouterLink, useRouter, useRoute, useSearchParams, useBlocker, useLoaderData, useIsActive, useTypedSearchParams, useTransition, useMiddlewareData, onBeforeRouteLeave, onBeforeRouteUpdate | Routing |
| @pyreon/head | useHead, HeadProvider, renderWithHead | Document head |
| @pyreon/server | createHandler, prerender, island, compileTemplate | SSR/SSG server |
| @pyreon/compiler | transformJSX, detectReactPatterns, migrateReactCode, per-text-node _bind, pure call detection, spread templates, reactive props inlining | Build tools |
| @pyreon/cli | doctor, context | Developer CLI |
| @pyreon/mcp | MCP server | AI tool integration |
| @pyreon/typescript | base, app, lib | TypeScript config presets |
| @pyreon/vite-plugin | pyreon() | Vite integration |
| @pyreon/storybook | renderToCanvas, Meta, StoryObj | Storybook renderer |
| @pyreon/lint | lint, lintFile, listRules, applyFixes, allRules, loadConfig, createIgnoreFilter, AstCache, watchAndLint, isPathExempt, isTestFile | Pyreon-specific linter (59 rules, 12 categories, config files, watch mode). Includes `pyreon/no-process-dev-gate` — auto-fixable, forbids `typeof process` dev gates that are dead code in real Vite browser bundles. Includes `pyreon/require-browser-smoke-test` — every browser-categorized package must ship at least one `*.browser.test.{ts,tsx}` under `src/`. Per-rule options via ESLint-style tuple form (`[severity, options]`); `RuleContext.getOptions()`; `RuleMeta.schema` validates user config; `LintResult.configDiagnostics` surfaces malformed-config errors to CI/LSP; JSON Schema at `@pyreon/lint/schema/pyreonlintrc.schema.json`; CLI `--rule-options id='{json}'`. Rule precision: `no-window-in-ssr` recognises `requestAnimationFrame` as a safe context, handles `watch(source, cb)` precisely (only 2nd-arg callback is safe — source still analysed), and handles logical-and guards, AND-chain + ternary const bindings, function-parameter shadowing, and module-level import-name shadowing (e.g. `import { history } from '@codemirror/commands'` makes every `history` the import); inline suppression accepts both `// pyreon-lint-ignore <id>` and `// pyreon-lint-disable-next-line <id>`; `no-bare-signal-in-jsx` skips VNode-producing helpers (`render`, `h`, `cloneVNode`); `no-imperative-navigate-in-render` tracks nested-function depth (deferred-execution callbacks don't false-fire; sync invocations of dangerous nested fns still fire); `no-dom-in-setup` safe contexts include `onUnmount`/`onCleanup`/`renderEffect`/`requestAnimationFrame`; `dev-guard-warnings` recognises `__DEV__`/`IS_DEV`/`IS_DEVELOPMENT`/`isDev` and accepts user-supplied `devFlagNames`. |
| @pyreon/store | defineStore, resetStore, addStorePlugin | Global state management |
| @pyreon/state-tree | model, getSnapshot, applySnapshot, onPatch, applyPatch | Structured reactive state tree |
| @pyreon/form | useForm, useField, useFieldArray, useWatch, FormProvider | Signal-based forms |
| @pyreon/validation | zodSchema, valibotSchema, arktypeSchema | Schema adapters for forms |
| @pyreon/query | useQuery, useMutation, useInfiniteQuery, useSuspenseQuery, useSubscription, useSSE | TanStack Query adapter |
| @pyreon/table | useTable, flexRender | TanStack Table adapter |
| @pyreon/virtual | useVirtualizer, useWindowVirtualizer | TanStack Virtual adapter |
| @pyreon/i18n | createI18n, I18nProvider, useI18n, Trans | Reactive i18n |
| @pyreon/feature | defineFeature, reference, extractFields | Schema-driven CRUD primitives |
| @pyreon/charts | useChart, Chart | Reactive ECharts bridge. **Vite + browser tests need a `tslib` alias** to `tslib.es6.js` (microsoft/tslib#189). Use `tslibBrowserAlias()` from `vitest.browser.ts` in test configs; add `resolve.alias: { tslib: 'tslib/tslib.es6.js' }` to consumer apps' `vite.config.ts`. |
| @pyreon/storage | useStorage, useSessionStorage, useCookie, useIndexedDB | Reactive client storage |
| @pyreon/hotkeys | useHotkey, useHotkeyScope, registerHotkey | Keyboard shortcuts |
| @pyreon/permissions | createPermissions, PermissionsProvider, usePermissions | Reactive permissions |
| @pyreon/machine | createMachine | Reactive state machines |
| @pyreon/flow | createFlow, useFlow, Flow, Background, MiniMap, Controls, Handle | Flow diagrams |
| @pyreon/code | createEditor, CodeEditor, DiffEditor, TabbedEditor | Code editor (CodeMirror 6) |
| @pyreon/document | render, createDocument, Document, Page, Heading, Text, Table | Universal document rendering |
| @pyreon/rx | filter, map, sortBy, groupBy, keyBy, uniqBy, take, skip, last, chunk, flatten, find, mapValues, count, sum, min, max, average, distinct, scan, combine, debounce, throttle, search, pipe | Signal-aware reactive transforms |
| @pyreon/toast | toast, toast.success, toast.error, toast.warning, toast.info, toast.loading, toast.update, toast.dismiss, toast.promise, Toaster | Toast notifications |
| @pyreon/url-state | useUrlState | URL-synced reactive state |
| @pyreon/ui-core | config, init, PyreonUI, useMode, Provider, get, set, merge, omit, pick, compose | UI config engine + unified provider |
| @pyreon/styler | styled, css, keyframes, createGlobalStyle, ThemeContext, useTheme | CSS-in-JS |
| @pyreon/unistyle | responsive breakpoints, CSS property mappings, unit utilities, enrichTheme | CSS utilities |
| @pyreon/hooks | useHover, useFocus, useBreakpoint, useElementSize, useFocusTrap, useMediaQuery, useScrollLock, useClipboard, useDialog, useTimeAgo, useOnline, useEventListener, useInfiniteScroll, 26+ more | Signal-based hooks |
| @pyreon/elements | Element, Text, List, Overlay, Portal | Base UI primitives |
| @pyreon/attrs | attrs() | Chainable HOC factory |
| @pyreon/rocketstyle | rocketstyle() | Multi-state styling system |
| @pyreon/coolgrid | Container, Row, Col | Responsive grid |
| @pyreon/kinetic | kinetic(), .preset(), .enter(), .leave(), .collapse(), .stagger() | CSS-transition animations |
| @pyreon/kinetic-presets | fade, slideUp, slideDown, scaleIn, 120+ presets | Animation presets |
| @pyreon/connector-document | extractDocumentTree(vnode) — invokes component functions to read post-attrs `_documentProps`, resolves function values for reactive metadata | Walks rocketstyle component tree → DocNode |
| @pyreon/document-primitives | DocDocument (reactive title/author/subject accessors), DocPage, DocHeading, DocText, DocList, DocTable, DocRow, DocColumn (18 total) + extractDocNode(fn) one-step helper | Same tree renders in browser AND exports to 14+ formats |

## @pyreon/reactivity — Complete API

### signal<T>(initialValue, options?)
```typescript
const count = signal(0)
count()                        // read (subscribes current effect/component)
count.peek()                   // read without subscribing
count.set(5)                   // set to value
count.update(n => n + 1)       // update with function
count.subscribe(fn)            // manual subscription, returns unsubscribe
count.direct(updater)          // compiler-generated DOM updater
```

### computed<T>(fn, options?)
```typescript
const doubled = computed(() => count() * 2)
doubled()    // auto-memoized, recomputes only when deps change
```

### effect(fn)
```typescript
const dispose = effect(() => {
  console.log(count())   // auto-tracks
  return () => { /* cleanup */ }
})
dispose()  // stop effect
```

### onCleanup(fn)
```typescript
// Register cleanup inside an effect (runs between re-executions):
effect(() => {
  const handler = () => console.log(count())
  window.addEventListener("resize", handler)
  onCleanup(() => window.removeEventListener("resize", handler))
})
```

### renderEffect(fn)
```typescript
// Lightweight effect for DOM binding (used by compiled templates)
const dispose = renderEffect(() => el.textContent = String(count()))
```

### batch(fn)
```typescript
batch(() => { a.set(1); b.set(2) })  // single notification
```

### nextTick()
```typescript
await nextTick()  // wait for current batch to complete
```

### createStore<T>(initialValue)
```typescript
const store = createStore({ user: { name: "Alice" }, items: [] })
store.user.name = "Bob"     // granular update
store.items.push("new")     // array methods work
```

### createResource<T>(fetcher, options?)
```typescript
const users = createResource(async () => {
  const res = await fetch("/api/users")
  return res.json()
})
users()           // data (or undefined while loading)
users.loading()   // boolean
users.error()     // Error | null
users.refetch()   // re-run fetcher
```

### createSelector(source, fn?)
```typescript
const isSelected = createSelector(selectedId)
// In list item:
const active = isSelected(item.id)  // reactive boolean
```

### watch(source, callback, options?)
```typescript
watch(count, (newVal, oldVal) => {
  console.log(`Changed from ${oldVal} to ${newVal}`)
})
```

### runUntracked(fn)
```typescript
// Read signals without subscribing:
const val = runUntracked(() => count())
```

### untrack(fn)
```typescript
// Alias for runUntracked — reads signals without subscribing:
import { untrack } from "@pyreon/reactivity"
const val = untrack(() => count())
```

## @pyreon/core — Complete API

### h(type, props, ...children)
```typescript
h("div", { class: "box" }, h("span", null, "Hello"))
h("div", { class: ["box", isActive && "active"] })  // class accepts ClassValue
h(MyComponent, { name: "world" })
h(Fragment, null, child1, child2)
```

### Components
```typescript
// Components are plain functions, run ONCE per lifetime
const Greeting = (props: { name: string }) => {
  return <h1>Hello, {props.name}</h1>
}

// With signals (reactive state):
const Counter = (props: { initial: number }) => {
  const count = signal(props.initial)
  const increment = () => count.update(n => n + 1)
  return <button onClick={increment}>Count: {count()}</button>
}

// Children:
const Layout = (props: { children: VNodeChild }) => {
  return <div class="layout">{props.children}</div>
}
```

### For
```typescript
// MUST use "by" (not "key") — JSX extracts key for VNode reconciliation
<For each={items()} by={item => item.id}>
  {(item, index) => <li>{index()}: {item.name}</li>}
</For>
```

### Show
```typescript
<Show when={condition()} fallback={<FallbackUI />}>
  <MainContent />
</Show>
```

### Switch / Match
```typescript
<Switch>
  <Match when={status() === "loading"}><Spinner /></Match>
  <Match when={status() === "error"}><ErrorDisplay /></Match>
  <Match when={status() === "ready"}><Content /></Match>
</Switch>
```

### Suspense
```typescript
<Suspense fallback={<div>Loading...</div>}>
  <LazyComponent />
</Suspense>
```

### lazy(loader, options?)
```typescript
const HeavyPage = lazy(() => import("./HeavyPage"))
const WithLoading = lazy(() => import("./Page"), {
  loading: () => <Spinner />,
  error: (err) => <ErrorView error={err} />,
})
```

### Dynamic
```typescript
<Dynamic component={currentComponent()} someProp="value" />
<Dynamic component="div" class="dynamic-tag" />
```

### Portal
```typescript
<Portal element={document.body}>
  <Modal>Content</Modal>
</Portal>
```

### ErrorBoundary
```typescript
<ErrorBoundary
  onCatch={(err) => logError(err)}
  fallback={(err, reset) => (
    <div>
      <p>Error: {err.message}</p>
      <button onClick={reset}>Retry</button>
    </div>
  )}
>
  <App />
</ErrorBoundary>
```

### Lifecycle Hooks
```typescript
// Optionally return a cleanup function (void is fine too):
onMount(() => {
  const id = setInterval(tick, 1000)
  return () => clearInterval(id)
})

onUnmount(() => {
  console.log("Removed from DOM")
})

onUpdate(() => {
  console.log("Component updated")
})

onErrorCaptured((err) => {
  console.error(err)
  return true  // stop propagation
})
```

### Context
```typescript
const ThemeCtx = createContext<"light" | "dark">("light")

// Provide (recommended — auto-cleans up on unmount):
provide(ThemeCtx, "dark")

// Low-level (manual cleanup):
pushContext(new Map([[ThemeCtx.id, "dark"]]))
onUnmount(() => popContext())

// Consume:
const theme = useContext(ThemeCtx)
```

### ExtractProps / HigherOrderComponent
```typescript
// Extract props type from a ComponentFn:
type Props = ExtractProps<typeof MyComponent>

// Typed HOC pattern (HOP = injected props, P = wrapped props):
function withTheme<P>(Wrapped: ComponentFn<P>): HigherOrderComponent<{ theme?: string }, P> {
  return (props) => <Wrapped {...props} />
}
```

### createRef
```typescript
const inputRef = createRef<HTMLInputElement>()
<input ref={inputRef} />
// After mount: inputRef.current.focus()
```

### cx(...values: ClassValue[]): string
```typescript
import { cx } from "@pyreon/core"

// Strings:
cx("foo", "bar")                  // "foo bar"

// Conditionals:
cx("base", isActive && "active")  // "base active" or "base"

// Objects:
cx({ base: true, active: isActive(), hidden: false })  // "base active"

// Arrays (nested):
cx(["base", ["nested", { active: true }]])  // "base nested active"

// class prop accepts ClassValue directly:
<div class="static" />
<div class={["a", condition() && "b"]} />
<div class={{ base: true, active: isActive() }} />
```

### splitProps(props, keys)
```typescript
import { splitProps } from "@pyreon/core"

const Button = (props: { class?: string; style?: StyleValue; onClick: () => void; children: VNodeChild }) => {
  const [local, rest] = splitProps(props, ["class", "style"])
  return <button {...rest} class={local.class} style={local.style} />
}
```

### mergeProps(...sources)
```typescript
import { mergeProps } from "@pyreon/core"

const Button = (props: { size?: string; variant?: string }) => {
  const merged = mergeProps({ size: "md", variant: "primary" }, props)
  return <button class={`btn-${merged.size} btn-${merged.variant}`} />
}
```

### createUniqueId()
```typescript
import { createUniqueId } from "@pyreon/core"

const Form = () => {
  const id = createUniqueId()  // "pyreon-1", "pyreon-2", etc.
  return (
    <div>
      <label for={id}>Name</label>
      <input id={id} />
    </div>
  )
}
```

### TargetedEvent<E>
```typescript
// Types currentTarget per element — no manual casts:
<input onInput={(e: TargetedEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
  console.log(e.currentTarget.value)  // string, fully typed
}} />

<select onChange={(e: TargetedEvent<HTMLSelectElement>) => {
  console.log(e.currentTarget.value)
}} />
```

## @pyreon/router — Complete API

### Route Definition
```typescript
interface RouteRecord {
  path: string                            // "/user/:id", "/file/(.*)", ":param?"
  component: ComponentFn | LazyComponent
  name?: string                           // for named navigation
  meta?: { title?: string; requiresAuth?: boolean; [key: string]: unknown }
  redirect?: string | ((route) => string)
  beforeEnter?: NavigationGuard | NavigationGuard[]
  beforeLeave?: NavigationGuard | NavigationGuard[]
  alias?: string | string[]               // alternative paths
  children?: RouteRecord[]                // nested routes
  loader?: (ctx: LoaderContext) => Promise<unknown>
  staleWhileRevalidate?: boolean          // show stale data while reloading
  errorComponent?: ComponentFn            // error UI for this route
}
```

### createRouter
```typescript
const router = createRouter({
  routes: [
    { path: "/", component: Home, name: "home" },
    { path: "/user/:id", component: User, name: "user",
      loader: async ({ params }) => {
        const res = await fetch(`/api/user/${params.id}`)
        return res.json()
      }
    },
    { path: "/admin", component: AdminLayout, beforeEnter: requireAuth, children: [
      { path: "", component: AdminDashboard },
      { path: "users", component: AdminUsers },
      { path: "settings", component: AdminSettings },
    ]},
  ],
  mode: "history",  // or "hash"
})
```

### Navigation
```typescript
const router = useRouter()

// Push (adds to history):
router.push("/settings")
router.push({ name: "user", params: { id: "42" } })
router.push({ path: "/search", query: { q: "hello" } })

// Replace (no history entry):
router.replace("/login")

// History navigation:
router.back()
router.forward()
router.go(-2)

// Wait for initial route resolution:
await router.isReady()
```

### Route Access
```typescript
const route = useRoute<"/user/:id">()
route().path          // "/user/42"
route().params.id     // "42" (typed!)
route().query         // { key: "value" }
route().meta          // { title: "User Profile" }
route().name          // "user"
```

### Search Params
```typescript
const [search, setSearch] = useSearchParams({ page: "1", sort: "name" })
search().page          // "1"
await setSearch({ page: "2" })  // updates URL query string
```

### Navigation Guards
```typescript
// Global:
router.beforeEach(async (to, from) => {
  if (to.meta.requiresAuth && !isAuthenticated()) return "/login"
})
router.afterEach((to, from) => {
  document.title = to.meta.title ?? "App"
})

// In-component:
onBeforeRouteLeave(async (to, from) => {
  if (hasUnsavedChanges()) return confirm("Discard changes?")
})
onBeforeRouteUpdate(async (to, from) => {
  // params changed, same component
  await refetchData(to.params.id)
})
```

### Navigation Blocking
```typescript
const { isBlocked, proceed, cancel } = useBlocker(
  () => formDirty()  // block when form has unsaved changes
)

<Show when={isBlocked()}>
  <dialog open>
    <p>You have unsaved changes.</p>
    <button onClick={proceed}>Leave</button>
    <button onClick={cancel}>Stay</button>
  </dialog>
</Show>
```

### Loader Data
```typescript
// Route:
{ path: "/posts", component: Posts,
  loader: async () => {
    const res = await fetch("/api/posts")
    return res.json()
  },
  staleWhileRevalidate: true  // show cached while refetching
}

// Component:
const Posts = () => {
  const posts = useLoaderData<Post[]>()
  return <For each={posts} by={p => p.id}>{p => <PostCard post={p} />}</For>
}
```

### Type-Safe Params
```typescript
type UserParams = ExtractParams<"/user/:id">  // { id: string }
type PostParams = ExtractParams<"/blog/:slug/comment/:commentId">  // { slug: string; commentId: string }
const path = buildPath("/user/:id", { id: "42" })  // "/user/42"
```

### Typed Search Params
```typescript
import { useTypedSearchParams } from '@pyreon/router'

const params = useTypedSearchParams({ page: 'number', q: 'string', active: 'boolean' })
params.page()    // number (auto-coerced from URL string)
params.q()       // string
params.active()  // boolean
params.set({ page: 2, q: 'hello' })  // updates URL
```

### Route Transitions
```typescript
import { useTransition } from '@pyreon/router'

const { isTransitioning } = useTransition()
// true during navigation (guards + loaders), false when route component mounted
```

### View Transitions API
```typescript
// Auto-enabled — route changes wrapped in document.startViewTransition()
// Opt out per route:
{ path: '/modal', component: Modal, meta: { viewTransition: false } }

// Style with CSS pseudo-elements:
// ::view-transition-old(root) { animation: fade-out 200ms ease-in }
// ::view-transition-new(root) { animation: fade-in 300ms ease-out }
```

**What `await router.push()` waits for:** the router awaits the ViewTransition's `updateCallbackDone` promise (DOM committed, new state live). It does NOT await `.finished` (full animation — 200-300ms), which would add unwanted latency to every programmatic navigation. `.ready` and `.finished` get empty `.catch()` handlers so their `AbortError: Transition was skipped` rejections (fired when a newer navigation interrupts an in-flight transition) don't leak as unhandled promise rejections.

| Promise | Resolves when | Router awaits? |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `updateCallbackDone` | Callback done; DOM swapped; state live | yes |
| `ready` | Snapshot captured, pseudo-elements ready | no — `.catch()` only |
| `finished` | Full animation completed | no — `.catch()` only |

**Hook ordering**: `afterEach` hooks and `scrollManager.restore` fire AFTER the VT callback completes — they observe the NEW route state, consistent with the hook's documented "after-navigation" semantics. (Pre-fix they fired between `commitNavigation` returning and the VT callback running, briefly observing OLD state. No internal consumer was affected; external apps using `router.afterEach()` for analytics now see the correct current route when the hook fires.)

### Hash Scrolling
```typescript
// Navigating to /docs#installation auto-scrolls to element with id="installation"
router.push('/docs#installation')
```

### Route Error Boundaries
```typescript
// errorComponent catches render errors (not just loader errors):
{
  path: '/dashboard',
  component: Dashboard,
  errorComponent: (props) => <div>Error: {props.error.message} <button onClick={props.reset}>Retry</button></div>,
}
```

### Middleware Chain
```typescript
import type { RouteMiddleware } from '@pyreon/router'

const auth: RouteMiddleware = async (ctx) => {
  ctx.data.user = await getUser(ctx.request)
  if (!ctx.data.user) return '/login'
}

// In routes:
{ path: '/admin', component: Admin, middleware: [auth] }

// In components:
import { useMiddlewareData } from '@pyreon/router'
const data = useMiddlewareData<{ user: User }>()
```

### Typed Route Names
```typescript
type Names = 'home' | 'user' | 'settings'
const router = createRouter<Names>({ routes: [...] })
router.push({ name: 'user', params: { id: '42' } })  // typed — only valid names
```

## @pyreon/head — Complete API

```typescript
useHead({
  title: "Page Title",
  titleTemplate: "{0} — My App",
  meta: [
    { name: "description", content: "Page description" },
    { property: "og:title", content: "Open Graph Title" },
  ],
  link: [
    { rel: "canonical", href: "https://example.com/page" },
    { rel: "stylesheet", href: "/styles.css" },
  ],
  script: [
    { src: "/analytics.js", async: true },
    { children: "console.log('inline')" },
  ],
  style: [{ children: "body { margin: 0 }" }],
  htmlAttrs: { lang: "en" },
  bodyAttrs: { class: "dark" },
  jsonLd: { "@type": "WebPage", name: "My Page" },
})
```

## @pyreon/server — Complete API

### createHandler
```typescript
const handler = createHandler({
  App: RootApp,
  routes: routeConfig,
  clientEntry: "/src/entry-client.ts",
  mode: "stream",
  middleware: [
    async (ctx, next) => {
      ctx.locals.user = await getUser(ctx.req)
      await next()
    }
  ],
})

// Use with any server:
Bun.serve({ fetch: handler })
```

### Island Architecture
```typescript
// Server component (SSR only):
const Page = () => (
  <div>
    <h1>Static content (SSR)</h1>
    <SearchBar query="initial" />  {/* hydrated island */}
  </div>
)

// Island definition:
const SearchBar = island(
  () => import("./components/SearchBar"),
  { name: "SearchBar", hydrate: "visible" }
)

// Client entry:
import { hydrateIslands } from "@pyreon/server/client"
hydrateIslands({
  SearchBar: () => import("./components/SearchBar"),
})
```

### Pre-rendering (SSG)
```typescript
import { prerender } from "@pyreon/server"

await prerender({
  handler,
  paths: ["/", "/about", "/blog/1", "/blog/2"],
  outDir: "./dist",
})
```

### Template Compilation (Performance)
```typescript
import { compileTemplate, processCompiledTemplate, buildClientEntryTag } from "@pyreon/server"

const compiled = compileTemplate(htmlTemplate)  // parse once
const html = processCompiledTemplate(compiled, {
  head: headTags,
  body: renderedApp,
  scripts: buildClientEntryTag("/src/entry-client.ts"),
})
```

## @pyreon/runtime-dom — Key APIs

### mount / hydrateRoot
```typescript
import { mount, hydrateRoot } from "@pyreon/runtime-dom"

// Client SPA:
mount(<App />, document.getElementById("app")!)

// Client hydration (after SSR):
hydrateRoot(<App />, document.getElementById("app")!)
```

### Transitions
```typescript
<Transition name="fade" mode="out-in"
  onBeforeEnter={el => { /* */ }}
  onAfterLeave={el => { /* */ }}
>
  <Show when={visible()}><div>Content</div></Show>
</Transition>

<TransitionGroup name="list" tag="ul">
  <For each={items()} by={i => i.id}>
    {item => <li>{item.name}</li>}
  </For>
</TransitionGroup>
```

### KeepAlive
```typescript
<KeepAlive>
  <Dynamic component={currentTab()} />
</KeepAlive>
```

## Vite Plugin Setup

```typescript
// vite.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "vite"
import { pyreon } from "@pyreon/vite-plugin"

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    pyreon({
      // SSR dev server:
      ssr: { entry: "./src/entry-server.ts" },
      // React compat mode (optional):
      // compat: "react",
    }),
  ],
})
```

## Compiler — Reactive Props Inlining

The compiler auto-detects `const` variables derived from `props.*` or `splitProps` results and inlines them at JSX use sites for automatic reactivity.

```typescript
// Input
function Greeting(props) {
  const x = props.name ?? 'World'
  return <div>{x}</div>
}

// Compiler output — x inlined as (props.name ?? 'World') inside _bind():
_tpl('<div></div>', (__root) => {
  const __t0 = document.createTextNode('')
  __root.appendChild(__t0)
  const __d0 = _bind(() => { __t0.data = (props.name ?? 'World') })
  return () => { __d0() }
})
```

- Props member access (`props.x`, `own.y` from splitProps) detected as reactive source
- Transitive: `const a = props.x; const b = a + 1` → `b` inlined as `((props.x) + 1)`
- Only `const` tracked — `let`/`var` are mutable, excluded from inlining
- Non-JSX usage stays static (`console.log(x)` uses captured value — correct)
- Works with splitProps: `const [own] = splitProps(props, ['x']); const v = own.x` → `v` inlined
- **Circular references are safe**: `const a = b + props.x; const b = a + 1` compiles without crashing — `resolveExprTransitive` uses a `visited: Set<string>` to break cycles. The cyclic identifier is left as-is (uses its captured value). The compiler emits a `circular-prop-derived` warning so the developer knows the cyclic part isn't reactively inlined.

## React → Pyreon Migration Cheat Sheet

| React | Pyreon | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `useState(0)` | `signal(0)` | Callable function, not destructured |
| `useEffect(fn, [])` | `onMount(fn)` | Must return undefined or cleanup |
| `useEffect(fn, [dep])` | `effect(fn)` | Auto-tracks, no dep array |
| `useMemo(fn, [deps])` | `computed(fn)` | Auto-tracks deps |
| `useCallback(fn, [deps])` | `fn` | Not needed, components run once |
| `useRef(null)` | `createRef()` | For DOM refs |
| `useRef(value)` | `signal(value)` | For mutable values |
| `useReducer` | `signal` + `update` | Pattern with update function |
| `useContext` | `useContext` | Same name, different import |
| `memo(Comp)` | `Comp` | Not needed, signals handle updates |
| `forwardRef(fn)` | `fn` | Pass ref as normal prop |
| `className` | `class` | Standard HTML |
| `htmlFor` | `for` | Standard HTML |
| `onChange` on input | `onInput` | Native DOM events |
| `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` | `innerHTML` | Direct prop |
| `items.map(...)` in JSX | `<For each={...} by={...}>` | Reactive list |
| `cond ? <A/> : <B/>` | `<Show when={cond}><A/></Show>` | Reactive conditional |
| `import from "react"` | `import from "@pyreon/core"` | Different package |
| `import from "react-dom"` | `import from "@pyreon/runtime-dom"` | Different package |
| `createRoot(el).render()` | `mount(<App/>, el)` | Simpler API |

## @pyreon/store — State Management

```typescript
import { defineStore } from '@pyreon/store'

const useCounter = defineStore('counter', () => {
  const count = signal(0)
  const doubled = computed(() => count() * 2)
  const increment = () => count.update(n => n + 1)
  return { count, doubled, increment }
})

// Usage:
const { store } = useCounter()
store.count()       // 0
store.increment()   // triggers reactive updates
store.doubled()     // 2

// Store API:
const api = useCounter()
api.patch({ count: 10 })       // partial update
api.subscribe((mutation) => {}) // watch changes
api.reset()                     // reset to initial
api.dispose()                   // cleanup
```

## @pyreon/form — Form Management

Signal-based form management for Pyreon. Each field (`value`, `error`, `touched`, `dirty`) is its own `Signal<T>` so templates only re-run for the slice they read. First-class schema validation (plug in `zodSchema` / `valibotSchema` / `arktypeSchema` from `@pyreon/validation`), per-field `validateOn: blur | change | submit`, async validators with optional `debounceMs` and version-based stale-result discarding, cross-field validation via `(value, allValues) => …`, dynamic `useFieldArray` with stable keys for keyed rendering, and typed `useWatch` overloads for single / multi / all-field reactive watchers.

```typescript
import { useForm, useField, useFieldArray, useWatch, useFormState, FormProvider } from '@pyreon/form'
import { zodSchema } from '@pyreon/validation/zod'
import { z } from 'zod'

// 1. useForm — entry point. initialValues is the single source of truth
//    for field keys + types. onSubmit receives validated values.
const form = useForm({
  initialValues: { email: '', password: '', remember: false, tags: [] as string[] },
  validators: {
    email: (v) => (!v ? 'Required' : undefined),
    // Cross-field: validator receives (value, allValues)
    password: (v, all) =>
      v.length < 8 ? 'Too short' : v === all.email ? 'Password must differ from email' : undefined,
  },
  schema: zodSchema(z.object({
    email: z.string().email(),
    password: z.string().min(8),
  })),
  validateOn: 'blur',    // 'blur' (default) | 'change' | 'submit'
  debounceMs: 300,       // optional — stale async results are discarded via version counter
  onSubmit: async (values) => { await api.login(values) },
})

// 2. register() — bind an input. Returns { value, onInput, onBlur } and,
//    for type: 'checkbox', also a `checked` accessor.
<form onSubmit={form.handleSubmit}>
  <input {...form.register('email')} />
  <input type="password" {...form.register('password')} />
  <input type="checkbox" {...form.register('remember', { type: 'checkbox' })} />
</form>

// 3. useField — extract one field's state for isolated components.
//    `hasError` / `showError` are computeds so you don't recompute
//    the touched-AND-error condition at every call site.
function EmailField({ form }: { form: typeof form }) {
  const field = useField(form, 'email')
  return (
    <>
      <input {...field.register()} />
      {() => field.showError() && <span class="error">{field.error()}</span>}
    </>
  )
}

// 4. useFieldArray — dynamic arrays with stable keys for <For>.
const tags = useFieldArray<string>(['typescript'])
tags.append('pyreon')
tags.prepend('signals')
tags.insert(1, 'reactive')
tags.move(0, 2)
tags.swap(0, 1)
tags.remove(0)
// tags.items() → FieldArrayItem<string>[] — { key, value: Signal<T> }
// Use the stable key in <For by={i => i.key}> so re-renders don't thrash.

// 5. useWatch — typed overloads: single field → Signal<T>,
//    multiple fields → [Signal<A>, Signal<B>], no args → Computed<TValues>.
const email = useWatch(form, 'email')          // Signal<string>
const [first, last] = useWatch(form, ['firstName', 'lastName'])
const everything = useWatch(form)              // Computed<TValues>

// 6. useFormState — derived form-level summary. Pass a selector to avoid
//    re-rendering when unrelated fields move.
const canSubmit = useFormState(form, (s) => s.isValid && !s.isSubmitting && s.isDirty)

// 7. FormProvider / useFormContext — skip prop-drilling across deep trees.
<FormProvider form={form}>
  <DeepInputs />
</FormProvider>
// Inside DeepInputs: const form = useFormContext<MyValues>()

// 8. Server errors — setFieldError / setErrors / clearErrors after a
//    failed submit. Does NOT touch touched state, so the error shows
//    regardless of blur status.
form.setErrors({ email: 'Already registered' })
```

> **validateOn default is `blur`, not `change`**: Fields validate on blur by default so users aren't scolded mid-keystroke. Use `validateOn: "change"` for instant feedback (often paired with `debounceMs: 300` to avoid thrashing async validators), or `validateOn: "submit"` for zero-feedback-until-submit forms. `showError` (from `useField`) gates on `touched`, so even with `validateOn: "change"` errors won't appear until the user has blurred at least once — this is intentional.
>
> **Field signals are independent**: Every `FieldState<T>` field (`value`, `error`, `touched`, `dirty`) is its own `Signal<T>` — reading `field.value()` does not subscribe to `field.error()`. Pair with `useField` so `hasError` / `showError` are computed once per field instead of recomputed at every call site.
>
> **Stable keys in `useFieldArray`**: `FieldArrayItem<T>.key` is a monotonically increasing number assigned at insert time — NOT the array index. Use `<For each={items()} by={(item) => item.key}>` so move / insert / remove preserve component identity and input focus. Index-based keys defeat the stable-key design and cause children to remount on every reorder.
>
> **Async validators + stale results**: Async validators are version-tracked: if the user types faster than the validator resolves, the stale result is discarded when it finally returns. Combine with `debounceMs` to also cut down the number of in-flight requests. The `isValidating` signal is true while any field has a pending async validation — use it to gate the submit button.
>
> **Server errors via `setFieldError` / `setErrors`**: After a failed submit, attach server-side errors with `form.setFieldError(name, msg)` or `form.setErrors({ email: "Taken" })`. These do NOT touch `touched` state, so errors display immediately regardless of blur status. `clearErrors()` wipes them on the next keystroke if `validateOn: "change"` is set, or on next submit otherwise.

## @pyreon/query — TanStack Query Adapter

Pyreon adapter for TanStack Query. Fine-grained signals per observer field (data, error, isFetching) so effects only re-run for the fields they read. Re-exports TanStack core (QueryClient, dehydrate/hydrate, etc.) so users import everything from `@pyreon/query`. Real-time hooks `useSubscription` (WebSocket, auto-reconnect, bidirectional) and `useSSE` (Server-Sent Events, read-only) share the QueryClient so cache invalidation from push updates is one line.

```typescript
import {
  QueryClient,
  QueryClientProvider,
  useQuery,
  useMutation,
  useInfiniteQuery,
  useSubscription,
  useSSE,
  useSuspenseQuery,
  QuerySuspense,
} from '@pyreon/query'

// 1. Create a QueryClient and mount the provider at the app root.
const client = new QueryClient()

const App = () => (
  <QueryClientProvider client={client}>
    <Content />
  </QueryClientProvider>
)

// 2. useQuery — `options` is a function so it can read Pyreon signals.
//    When the signal changes (e.g. a reactive queryKey), the observer
//    updates and refetches automatically.
const userId = signal(1)
const user = useQuery(() => ({
  queryKey: ['user', userId()],
  queryFn: () => fetch(`/api/users/${userId()}`).then((r) => r.json()),
}))
// user.data(), user.error(), user.isFetching() — each is its own signal,
// so a template that reads only isFetching won't re-run when data changes.

// 3. useMutation — reactive pending/success/error state + mutate/mutateAsync.
const create = useMutation({
  mutationFn: (input: CreatePostInput) =>
    fetch('/api/posts', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(input) }).then((r) => r.json()),
  onSuccess: () => client.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['posts'] }),
})
// <button onClick={() => create.mutate({ title: 'New' })}>Create</button>

// 4. useSubscription — reactive WebSocket with auto-reconnect. The
//    onMessage callback receives the QueryClient so push updates can
//    invalidate or directly patch cached queries.
const sub = useSubscription({
  url: 'wss://api.example.com/feed',
  onMessage: (event, queryClient) => {
    const payload = JSON.parse(event.data)
    if (payload.type === 'post-created') {
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['posts'] })
    }
  },
})
// sub.status() — 'connecting' | 'connected' | 'disconnected' | 'error'
// sub.send(data), sub.close(), sub.reconnect()

// 5. useSSE — same pattern as useSubscription but read-only (no send).
//    `parse` deserializes per message; `events` filters named event types.
const sse = useSSE({
  url: '/api/events',
  parse: JSON.parse,
  onMessage: (data, queryClient) => {
    if (data.type === 'order-updated') {
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['orders'] })
    }
  },
})
// sse.data() — last parsed message. sse.lastEventId() honours SSE `id` field.

// 6. Suspense — useSuspenseQuery narrows `data` to Signal<TData> (never
//    undefined). Pair with QuerySuspense to gate children on success.
const profile = useSuspenseQuery(() => ({
  queryKey: ['profile', userId()],
  queryFn: fetchProfile,
}))

<QuerySuspense
  query={profile}
  fallback={<Spinner />}
  error={(err) => <ErrorCard message={String(err)} />}
>
  {() => <ProfileCard name={profile.data().name} />}
</QuerySuspense>

// 7. useInfiniteQuery — reactive pages + fetchNextPage / fetchPreviousPage.
const feed = useInfiniteQuery(() => ({
  queryKey: ['feed'],
  queryFn: ({ pageParam }) => fetchPage(pageParam),
  initialPageParam: 0,
  getNextPageParam: (last) => last.nextCursor,
}))
```

> **Options as a function**: `useQuery` / `useInfiniteQuery` / `useQueries` / `useSuspenseQuery` take options as a FUNCTION (not an object) so `queryKey` and other fields can read Pyreon signals. TanStack core uses an object; Pyreon wraps so changing a tracked signal re-runs the observer options and refetches automatically.
>
> **Signals all the way down**: `result.data`, `.error`, `.isFetching`, etc. are independent `Signal<T>` values — not plain properties. Call them (`user.data()`) to read, and each field-level read only subscribes to that field so templates re-render with maximum precision.
>
> **Real-time + cache**: `useSubscription` (WebSocket) and `useSSE` (Server-Sent Events) both hand their `onMessage` callback the active `QueryClient`. Invalidate or patch queries directly from push updates instead of duplicating server state in a parallel signal store.
>
> **Suspense data is non-undefined**: `useSuspenseQuery` narrows `data: Signal<TData>` (never undefined). Read it unconditionally inside `QuerySuspense` children — the boundary guarantees success before rendering. Outside the boundary, use `useQuery` and handle the undefined case.

## @pyreon/permissions — Access Control

```typescript
import { createPermissions } from '@pyreon/permissions'

const can = createPermissions({
  'posts.read': true,
  'posts.create': true,
  'posts.delete': (post) => post.authorId === currentUser.id,
  'admin.*': false,
})

can('posts.read')           // true (reactive)
can('posts.delete', post)   // evaluates predicate
can.not('admin.dashboard')  // true
can.all('posts.read', 'posts.create')  // true
can.any('admin.users', 'posts.read')   // true
can.set({ 'admin.*': true })           // update reactively
```

## @pyreon/machine — State Machines

```typescript
import { createMachine } from '@pyreon/machine'

const traffic = createMachine({
  initial: 'red',
  states: {
    red:    { on: { NEXT: 'green' } },
    green:  { on: { NEXT: 'yellow' } },
    yellow: { on: { NEXT: 'red' } },
  },
})

traffic()              // 'red' (reactive signal)
traffic.send('NEXT')   // 'green'
traffic.matches('green') // true (reactive)
traffic.can('NEXT')    // true
traffic.nextEvents()   // ['NEXT']
traffic.reset()        // back to 'red'
```

## @pyreon/document — Universal Rendering

```typescript
import { createDocument, render } from '@pyreon/document'

// Builder pattern:
const doc = createDocument({ title: 'Report' })
  .heading('Q4 Sales')
  .text('Revenue increased by 25%.')
  .table({ columns: ['Region', 'Revenue'], rows: [['US', '$1M'], ['EU', '$800K']] })

await doc.toPdf()       // PDF (pdfmake)
await doc.toDocx()      // Word (docx)
await doc.toEmail()     // Outlook-safe HTML
await doc.toSlack()     // Slack Block Kit JSON
await doc.toNotion()    // Notion blocks
await doc.toMarkdown()  // Markdown

// JSX primitives:
<Document title="Report">
  <Page><Heading level={1}>Title</Heading><Text>Body</Text></Page>
</Document>
```

## @pyreon/flow — Flow Diagrams

Reactive flow diagrams for Pyreon. Signal-native nodes and edges, pan/zoom via pointer events + CSS transforms, auto-layout via lazy-loaded elkjs. No D3 dependency. Each node mounts exactly once across the lifetime of the graph; drags and selection patches are O(1) via per-node reactive accessors, so a 60fps drag in a 1000-node graph stays cheap.

```typescript
import { createFlow, useFlow, Flow, Background, Controls, MiniMap, Handle, Position, type NodeComponentProps } from '@pyreon/flow'

// createFlow is generic over node `data` shape — typed consumers
// pass their data type explicitly and `node.data.kind` narrows
// correctly without any index signature on the data interface.
interface WorkflowData {
  kind: 'trigger' | 'filter' | 'transform' | 'notify'
  label: string
}

const flow = createFlow<WorkflowData>({
  nodes: [
    { id: '1', type: 'custom', position: { x: 0, y: 0 }, data: { kind: 'trigger', label: 'Start' } },
    { id: '2', type: 'custom', position: { x: 200, y: 100 }, data: { kind: 'notify', label: 'End' } },
  ],
  edges: [{ id: 'e1', source: '1', target: '2', animated: true }],
})

flow.addNode({ id: '3', type: 'custom', position: { x: 100, y: 200 }, data: { kind: 'transform', label: 'New' } })
flow.addEdge({ source: '1', target: '3' })
await flow.layout('layered', { direction: 'RIGHT', nodeSpacing: 50, layerSpacing: 100 })  // lazy-loaded elkjs
// `direction`/`layerSpacing`/`edgeRouting` apply to layered/tree only —
// `force`/`stress`/`radial`/`box`/`rectpacking` silently ignore them.
// `nodeSpacing` is the only LayoutOptions field respected by every algorithm.

// Prefer `useFlow` inside components — it's `createFlow` + auto-dispose
// on unmount. Use `createFlow` only for singleton/app-store flows that
// outlive the component tree.
const MyDiagram = () => {
  const flow = useFlow<WorkflowData>({ nodes: [], edges: [] })
  return <Flow instance={flow}><Background /></Flow>
}

// Custom node renderers — every prop except `id` is a REACTIVE
// ACCESSOR (`() => T`), not a plain value. Read inside reactive
// scopes (JSX expression thunks, effect, computed) so the node
// patches in place when ANY underlying state changes. Each node
// mounts EXACTLY ONCE across the lifetime of the graph regardless
// of how many drags, selection clicks, or updateNode mutations
// happen — internally <Flow> uses <For> keyed by node.id plus
// per-node accessors that read live state from instance.nodes().
function CustomNode(props: NodeComponentProps<WorkflowData>) {
  return (
    <div
      class={() => (props.selected() ? 'selected' : '')}
      style={() => `cursor: ${props.dragging() ? 'grabbing' : 'grab'}`}
    >
      <Handle type="target" position={Position.Left} />
      {() => props.data().label}
      <Handle type="source" position={Position.Right} />
    </div>
  )
}

<Flow instance={flow} nodeTypes={{ custom: CustomNode }}>
  <Background variant="dots" gap={20} />
  <Controls position="bottom-left" />
  <MiniMap nodeColor={(node) => '#6366f1'} />
</Flow>

// Serialization round-trips for sidecar editors / persistence:
const json = flow.toJSON()         // { nodes, edges, viewport }
flow.fromJSON({ nodes, edges })    // restore from saved state
```

> **Peer dep**: @pyreon/runtime-dom
>
> **Note**: LayoutOptions.direction / layerSpacing / edgeRouting apply to layered/tree only — force/stress/radial/box/rectpacking silently ignore them. nodeSpacing is the only field respected by every algorithm. Dev-mode console.warn fires when an option is set on an algorithm that ignores it.
>
> **Mount once**: Each node mounts exactly once across the lifetime of the graph — drags, selection, and updateNode mutations patch via per-node reactive accessors, not remount.
>
> **Peer dep rationale**: `@pyreon/runtime-dom` is required in consumer apps because flow JSX components emit `_tpl()` / `_bind()` calls — declare it as a direct dependency, not a transitive one.
>
> **JSX generics**: Pyreon JSX components cannot be parameterised at the call site (`<Flow<MyData> />` is not valid JSX). `FlowProps.instance` is typed as `FlowInstance<any>` so typed consumers can pass their `FlowInstance<MyData>` without casting.

## @pyreon/storage — Persistent Reactive Storage

```typescript
import { useStorage, useCookie, useIndexedDB } from '@pyreon/storage'

const theme = useStorage('theme', 'light')   // localStorage, cross-tab synced
theme()           // 'light'
theme.set('dark') // persists + notifies other tabs
theme.remove()    // delete from storage

const token = useCookie('session', '', { maxAge: 3600, secure: true })
const cache = useIndexedDB('large-data', defaultValue, { dbName: 'app' })
```

## @pyreon/i18n — Internationalization

```typescript
// Full entry — includes JSX components (Trans, I18nProvider, useI18n)
import { createI18n, I18nProvider, useI18n, Trans } from '@pyreon/i18n'

const i18n = createI18n({
  locale: 'en',
  messages: { en: { greeting: 'Hello, {{name}}!', items_one: '{{count}} item', items_other: '{{count}} items' } },
  loader: (locale, ns) => import(`./locales/${locale}/${ns}.json`),
})

const { t, locale } = useI18n()
t('greeting', { name: 'World' })  // "Hello, World!"
t('items', { count: 5 })          // "5 items"
locale.set('fr')                   // switch locale reactively
```

```typescript
// Backend / non-JSX entry — `@pyreon/i18n/core`
// Zero JSX dependencies, transitively only depends on @pyreon/reactivity.
// Use this on backends, edge workers, non-Pyreon frontends, or any
// context where you don't need the <Trans> JSX component.
import { createI18n, interpolate, resolvePluralCategory } from '@pyreon/i18n/core'

const i18n = createI18n({
  locale: 'en',
  messages: { en: { hello: 'Hi {{name}}' } },
})
i18n.t('hello', { name: 'Backend' })  // "Hi Backend"
i18n.locale.set('de')  // reactive locale switching works the same
```

> **Two entry points**: `@pyreon/i18n` (full, with JSX) vs `@pyreon/i18n/core` (framework-agnostic). Both return identical `I18nInstance` objects, so you can switch entries later if you decide to add UI bindings. The main entry has a `/** @jsxImportSource @pyreon/core */` pragma on `trans.tsx` so even bun runtimes whose tsconfig has no `jsxImportSource` set still compile it correctly.

## @pyreon/code — Code Editor

```typescript
import { createEditor, CodeEditor, DiffEditor } from '@pyreon/code'
import { effect, signal } from '@pyreon/reactivity'

const editor = createEditor({
  value: '// hello',
  language: 'typescript',
  theme: 'dark',
  minimap: true,
  onChange: (next) => console.log('user edited:', next),
})

editor.value()              // reactive read
editor.value.set('new')     // write back into CodeMirror
editor.cursor()             // { line, col }
editor.lineCount()          // computed
editor.goToLine(42)
editor.insert('new code')
editor.setDiagnostics([{ from: 0, to: 5, severity: 'error', message: '...' }])

<CodeEditor instance={editor} style="height: 400px" />
<DiffEditor original="old code" modified="new code" language="typescript" />

// Two-way binding via `bindEditorToSignal` — replaces the recurring
// flag-based loop-prevention pattern. The helper handles both the
// external→editor and editor→external directions, with built-in
// loop prevention and parse-error handling.
import { bindEditorToSignal } from '@pyreon/code'

interface Doc { name: string; count: number }
const data = signal<Doc>({ name: 'Alice', count: 1 })

const editor = createEditor({
  value: JSON.stringify(data(), null, 2),
  language: 'json',
})

const binding = bindEditorToSignal({
  editor,
  signal: data,                // accepts Signal<T> or any SignalLike<T>
  serialize: (val) => JSON.stringify(val, null, 2),
  parse: (text) => {
    try { return JSON.parse(text) } catch { return null }
  },
  onParseError: (err) => console.warn('parse error:', err.message),
})

// Later — typically in onCleanup / onUnmount:
binding.dispose()

// What the helper guarantees:
//   1. No infinite loops — internal flags break the format-on-input race
//   2. Parse failures don't crash — external state stays at last valid value
//   3. Both effects are disposable via binding.dispose()
//
// What the helper does NOT do:
//   - It does NOT debounce. Every keystroke that parses successfully
//     triggers signal.set(parsed). Wrap in your own debounced layer if needed.
//   - It does NOT preserve cursor position when serialize re-canonicalizes
//     (e.g. reformatting). Make serialize() deterministic to avoid this.
```

> **Peer dep**: `@pyreon/runtime-dom` — `<CodeEditor>` JSX emits `_tpl()` calls. Consumer apps must declare it.

## @pyreon/rx — Reactive Transforms

```typescript
import { filter, map, sortBy, groupBy, pipe, debounce, throttle, search, sum, count } from '@pyreon/rx'

const items = signal([
  { name: 'Alice', age: 30 },
  { name: 'Bob', age: 25 },
  { name: 'Charlie', age: 35 },
])

// Signal input → Computed output (auto-tracks):
const adults = filter(items, i => i.age >= 30)       // Computed<Item[]>
const sorted = sortBy(items, 'name')                  // Computed<Item[]>
const grouped = groupBy(items, i => i.age > 30 ? 'senior' : 'junior')  // Computed<Record<string, Item[]>>
const total = sum(items, i => i.age)                   // Computed<number>
const n = count(items)                                 // Computed<number>

// Plain input → plain output:
const plainFiltered = filter([1, 2, 3], n => n > 1)   // [2, 3]

// Pipe — compose transforms left-to-right:
const result = pipe(
  items,
  filter(i => i.age >= 25),
  sortBy('name'),
  map(i => i.name),
)  // Computed<string[]>

// Time-based:
const debouncedSearch = debounce(searchQuery, 300)
const throttledPos = throttle(mousePos, 16)

// Text search:
const matches = search(items, query, { keys: ['name'] })
```

## @pyreon/toast — Toast Notifications

```typescript
import { toast, Toaster } from '@pyreon/toast'

// Imperative API — returns toast ID:
const id = toast('Hello!')
toast.success('Saved successfully')
toast.error('Something went wrong')
toast.warning('Check your input')
toast.info('New version available')

// Loading → success/error pattern:
const loadingId = toast.loading('Saving...')
toast.update(loadingId, { type: 'success', message: 'Done!' })

// Promise helper — auto-transitions:
toast.promise(saveData(), {
  loading: 'Saving...',
  success: 'Saved!',
  error: 'Failed to save',
})

// Dismiss:
toast.dismiss(id)    // dismiss one
toast.dismiss()      // dismiss all

// Render in your app (once):
const App = () => (
  <>
    <Toaster />  {/* Portal, a11y: role="alert" aria-live="polite" */}
    <MyApp />
  </>
)
```

## @pyreon/url-state — URL-Synced State

```typescript
import { useUrlState } from '@pyreon/url-state'

// Single param — synced to ?page=:
const page = useUrlState('page', 1)
page()       // 1 (auto-coerced from URL string to number)
page.set(2)  // updates URL to ?page=2 via replaceState

// Schema mode — multiple params:
const filters = useUrlState({ page: 1, sort: 'name', desc: false })
filters.page()   // 1
filters.sort()   // "name"
filters.desc()   // false (auto-coerced boolean)
filters.set({ page: 2, sort: 'date' })  // updates ?page=2&sort=date&desc=false

// SSR-safe — reads from request URL on server, window.location on client
```

## @pyreon/router — useIsActive

```typescript
import { useIsActive } from '@pyreon/router'

// Reactive boolean for active route matching:
const isHome = useIsActive('/')
const isAdmin = useIsActive('/admin')          // matches /admin, /admin/users, etc.
const isExactAdmin = useIsActive('/admin', true)  // exact match only

// Use in templates:
<nav>
  <a class={{ active: isHome() }} href="/">Home</a>
  <a class={{ active: isAdmin() }} href="/admin">Admin</a>
</nav>
```

## @pyreon/ui-core — PyreonUI Provider

### PyreonUI
```typescript
import { PyreonUI } from "@pyreon/ui-core"
import { enrichTheme } from "@pyreon/unistyle"

// Unified provider — replaces ThemeProvider + ModeProvider + ConfigProvider
const theme = enrichTheme({ colors: { primary: "#3b82f6" } })

const App = () => (
  <PyreonUI theme={theme} mode="system">
    <MyApp />
  </PyreonUI>
)

// Props:
// - theme: Theme object (use enrichTheme() to merge with defaults)
// - mode: "light" | "dark" | "system" (default: "light")
// - inversed: boolean (flips resolved mode — light↔dark)

// mode="system" auto-detects OS preference via prefers-color-scheme
// and reactively updates when the user changes their OS setting.
```

### useMode()
```typescript
import { useMode } from "@pyreon/ui-core"

const MyComponent = () => {
  const mode = useMode()  // Signal<"light" | "dark">

  return <div class={mode() === "dark" ? "bg-dark" : "bg-light"}>
    Current mode: {mode()}
  </div>
}
```
Returns the resolved mode as a reactive signal. When `mode="system"`, this reflects the OS preference. When `inversed` is true, the mode is flipped.

### enrichTheme(theme)
```typescript
import { enrichTheme } from "@pyreon/unistyle"

// Merges user theme with default breakpoints, spacing, and unit utilities:
const theme = enrichTheme({
  colors: { primary: "#3b82f6", secondary: "#6366f1" },
  fonts: { body: "Inter, sans-serif" },
})

<PyreonUI theme={theme} mode="dark">
  <App />
</PyreonUI>
```

### init() — Custom Environments
```typescript
import { init } from "@pyreon/ui-core"

// PyreonUI calls init() internally, but for custom setups (e.g., tests,
// SSR without PyreonUI, or non-standard environments) you can call it directly:
init({ theme, mode: "dark" })
```

## AI Developer Tools

### pyreon doctor
```bash
pyreon doctor              # scan for issues (human-readable)
pyreon doctor --json       # structured output for AI agents
pyreon doctor --fix        # auto-fix React patterns
pyreon doctor --ci         # exit code 1 on errors
```

### pyreon context
```bash
pyreon context             # generates .pyreon/context.json
```

Output: routes, components (with props/signals), islands, framework version.

### MCP Server
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "pyreon": {
      "command": "bunx",
      "args": ["@pyreon/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

Tools: get_api, validate, migrate_react, diagnose, get_routes, get_components

## Dev-mode Warnings: Pattern × Bundler Tree-shake Matrix

Pyreon dev warnings are gated on `import.meta.env?.DEV`. Tree-shake behavior depends on both the source pattern and the consumer bundler:

| Source pattern | Vite prod | Raw esbuild prod | Test |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `if (!import.meta.env?.DEV) return` (inline early-return) | tree-shaken | tree-shaken | `flow/src/tests/integration.test.ts` (esbuild) |
| `const __DEV__ = import.meta.env?.DEV === true; if (__DEV__) ...` | tree-shaken | mostly tree-shaken | `runtime-dom/src/tests/dev-gate-treeshake.test.ts` (Vite) |
| `const __DEV__ = ...; __DEV__ && cond && warn(...)` (chained &&) | tree-shaken | runtime-gated only | `runtime-dom/.../dev-gate-treeshake.test.ts` (Vite + non-Vite runtime smoke) |
| `typeof process !== 'undefined' && process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'` | dead in browser | dead in browser | `pyreon/no-process-dev-gate` lint rule |

Vite is Pyreon's primary supported bundler. Non-Vite consumers (webpack, bunchee, raw esbuild bundles) using the chained `&&` form may retain warning strings as data, but the runtime gate (`import.meta.env?.DEV === true`) evaluates to `false` when `import.meta.env.DEV` is undefined — warnings don't fire. Only a small bundle-size cost.

The runtime-dom tree-shake test bundles via Vite's `build()` API (not raw esbuild) because Vite is the consumer pipeline. Raw `esbuild --minify` cannot propagate a module-scope `const __DEV__` through chained `&&` patterns.
